description_long: This object is a stylized red flower with multiple broad, overlapping petals arranged in a radial pattern, forming a rounded bloom. The petals are thick and slightly pointed at the tips, creating a layered, whorled appearance. Emerging from the center are several long, slender, curved filaments that resemble stamens, adding vertical interest and a sense of delicacy. Around the base of the flower, there are a few flat, greenish-brown sepals or leaves that extend outward, providing contrast to the vibrant red petals. The flower lacks a visible stem, and the structure appears compact and low to the ground. The surface of the petals is matte and smooth, with a soft, semi-flexible look typical of fresh plant tissue.
description: A stylized red flower with broad, overlapping petals and long, curved stamens emerging from the center, surrounded by flat greenish-brown leaves at the base. The bloom is compact, rounded, and lacks a visible stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full spread of red petals, central stamens, and several greenish-brown leaves at the base.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the layered petal arrangement and the outward curve of the stamens.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the rounded shape of the bloom and the arching stamens, with leaves visible at the base.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back petals and the distribution of the base leaves, with stamens arching forward.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled pattern with significant overlapping and layering, forming a symmetrical, rounded bloom typical of many ornamental flowers.
